verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 is a type I integral membrane protein (TM-anchored) canonically resident in the trans-Golgi network (TGN), an endomembrane compartment. However, the NCBI summary explicitly documents cycling between early endosomes and the TGN—a dual-localization pattern. While the dominant steady-state distribution is endomembrane-resident (TGN), the documented secretory cycling via early endosomes establishes a minority PM pool en route between compartments. This qualifies as contextual via dual_localization: the protein has its own membrane anchor (type I TM) and transits the PM as part of constitutive vesicular cycling between intracellular compartments, even though the surface residence is transient and non-dominant.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The magnitude and detectability of the PM pool during secretory cycling is uncertain. If the surface transit is extremely rapid and the surface pool is negligible, the verdict might be no/endomembrane_resident instead. However, documented cycling between TGN and early endosomes is taken as evidence of PM transit during that cycling, warranting contextual.
